---
title: "Creating A Document Upload Form"
---

---
title: "Creating a Document Upload Form"
source: "https://support.hyland.com/r/Current/Hyland-Intelligent-Document-Processing-IDP/ydk1745915018470"
section: "Hyland Intelligent Document Processing (IDP)"
---

Forms are used to capture data into specially designed field types such as text, date, file uploads and multiple choice radio buttons. They are where human intervention is required within a process and this intervention is handled by filling in a form that is displayed in a user’s task list.

For more information, on configuring forms, see the corresponding sections in the Hyland Automate Modeler documentation: [Forms](https://support.hyland.com/access?dita:topicPath=fuh1673532219357.dita)

To create a document upload form:

1.  Next to Document AI Process, click Create. The Create widget is displayed.
2.  Select Create > Form. The Create Form dialog box is displayed.
3.  In the Form Name field, type Upload Form.
4.  Click Create Form. The form is created and displayed on new tab.
5.  In the form tab, drag and drop the File Upload ![](data:image/gif;base64,) element to the Add tab area.  Note: You can adjust the layout of the form, as required.
6.  Select the File Upload element and in the Advanced tabbed area, select the Allow multiple file attachments check box.
